summary: Iteration 3 backend testing: selfie upload (object storage) + match chat + swipe match_id. 18/18 tests passing (100%). Created /app/backend/tests/test_iteration3.py with 6 classes covering: auth-gating, MIME validation, 8MB cap, real object-storage upload+download round-trip, match_id presence on like-to-seed, foreign-user 404 isolation, two-message guarantee from seed auto-reply, chronological ordering, since-filter, and inbound read-flag toggling. JPEG bytes downloaded back equal bytes uploaded -- real Emergent object storage path is fully working.

minor.issue: since query param is compared as raw STRING against stored ISO created_at. If a client sends the timezone suffix '+00:00' un-encoded (i.e. literal '+' in querystring), FastAPI/Starlette decodes '+' as space, producing a value lexically < the stored string, so the strict $gt filter returns messages it should have excluded. Backend should parse `since` as datetime (Pydantic / fastapi.Query type) and either compare as datetime or accept urlencoded variants. Test was updated to use params={'since': ...} so '+' is %2B-encoded, but real frontend clients can hit this if they build the URL with f-strings.
Endpoint is public (no auth) by design — anyone with a path can fetch the bytes. That matches the spec (' direct'), but consider signed URLs / per-user access checks for private selfies in future.

action_items: Optional: switch `since` to `Optional[datetime] = Query(None)` so FastAPI parses ISO safely and the strict-gt comparison is timezone-aware. Low priority — frontend can avoid by URL-encoding.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 750 lines and mixes auth/discover/upload/chat — recommend splitting into routers/{auth,discover,chat,upload}.py after this iteration.

send_message stores created_at as ISO string; messages collection lacks a (match_id, created_at) compound index in ensure_indexes() — already partially indexed but worth verifying performance once message volume grows.
storage.put_object refresh-key path: globals()['_storage_key']=None then re-init — fine but consider a lock to avoid two concurrent requests both invalidating the key.

list_messages marks inbound as read AFTER reading; the returned message objects still show their pre-update read value. Frontend should treat the list call as side-effecting (current behaviour OK, just document).
test_credentials: /app/memory/test_credentials.md not present — iteration creates ad-hoc users iter3a+<ts>@example.com / crushme123 etc.
seed_data_creation: Module-scoped fixtures create user_a (iter3a), user_b (iter3b). Match created via swipe-like on a seed profile picked from /discover. No global seed mutated.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 3 endpoints all green. The only latent concern is `since=` URL-decoding: frontend MUST send the param via a proper query-param builder (e.g. URLSearchParams) so '+' is %2B-encoded; otherwise the strict $gt filter returns stale messages. Object storage upload+download verified end-to-end (real Emergent storage, not mocked). Iterations 1 + 2 not re-run per instructions.
rca of the issue: During first run, test_since_filter_returns_only_newer failed because the test embedded the ISO timestamp directly into f-string URL (f'?since={cutoff}'). The '+' in '+00:00' is interpreted as space by the URL-decoder server-side, producing a lexically-smaller 'since' than what's stored, so $gt incorrectly matched the auto-reply. Reproduction: GET /api/matches/{m}/messages?since=2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00 returns msgs at that exact instant. Mitigation in test: switched to params={'since': cutoff} so requests-lib percent-encodes '+'. Backend mitigation (recommended): declare `since: Optional[datetime] = Query(None)` so FastAPI parses ISO strictly and compares as datetime objects.
